title: Ensure users not logged on for 90 days or longer are disabled  for console logon

input_row.title: Ensure users not logged on for 90 days or longer are disabled  for console logon
input_row.description: Alibaba Cloud RAM users can logon to Alibaba Cloud console by using their user name and password. If a user has not logged on for 90 days or longer, it is recommended to disable the console access of the user.
input_row.rationale: Disabling users from having unnecessary logon privileges will reduce the opportunity that an abandoned user or a user with compromised password to be used.
input_row.audit: Perform the following to determine if a user has not logged on for 90 days or longer: Using the management console: 1. Logon RAM console. 2. Choose Identities > Users. 3. In the User Logon Name/Display Name column, click the username of each RAM user. 4. In the Console Logon Management section, check the latest logon time of each user in the Last Console Logon field. 5. Make sure that each user does not have a last console logon time dated earlier than 90 days ago.
input_row.remediation: Perform the following to disable console logon for a user: Using the management console: 1. Logon to RAM console. 2. Choose Identities > Users. 3. In the User Logon Name/Display Name column, click the username of the target RAM user. 4. In the Console Logon Management section, click Modify Logon Settings. 5. In the Console Password Logon section, select Disabled. 6. Click OK. Using the CLI aliyun ram DeleteLoginProfile --UserName
input_row.impact: RAM users who still need to log on to the management console or other Alibaba Cloud sites may encounter logon failure.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The audit can be fully verified via the Alibaba Cloud APIs/Aliyun CLI, as the control specifically involves checking the last logon time of RAM users, which is accessible through the Alibaba Cloud RAM API.

**PROGRAM NAME:** ali_ram_user_console_logon_disabled_if_inactive_90_days

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- The automated program would check the last logon time of each RAM user.
- Use the Alibaba Cloud CLI command: 
  ```bash
  aliyun ram ListUsers
  ```
  This command retrieves the list of all RAM users, including their login profiles.
- For each user retrieved, check the `LastConsoleLogon` attribute to determine the last date they logged into the console.
- The API call to validate if a user can log in to the console:
  ```bash
  aliyun ram GetLoginProfile --UserName <username>
  ```
  Verify the `LastConsoleLogon` date.
- Specific resources to enumerate include RAM user accounts.
- Pass/Fail criteria:
  - If `LastConsoleLogon` for a user is more than 90 days from the current date, they should be marked as non-compliant (logon should be disabled).
  - If the `LastConsoleLogon` is within the last 90 days, the user is compliant.
  
- Example logic:
  - If the last logon date for a RAM user is older than 90 days:
    - Check if their console access is disabled, if not, mark as non-compliant.
  - If within the last 90 days, mark as compliant. 

This fully automated approach will ensure continuous compliance monitoring for the specified control without requiring manual intervention.
